# UC-22.2.28 ¬∑ NIS2 Cyber Hygiene Practices ‚Äî Baseline Control Compliance

## Description

Scores each in-scope row from `nis2_asset_hygiene_expectations.csv` against Tenable.sc asset export fields to reveal missing multi-factor markers or endpoint protection plugins so hygiene backlogs surface before auditors ask for screenshots.

## Value

Cyber hygiene is not a single patch Tuesday‚Äîit is an ongoing assurance that baseline protections exist on every system supporting essential services; rolling that proof into Splunk prevents the last-minute scramble of logging into Tenable manually for each subsidiary.

## Implementation

(1) Keep `nis2_asset_hygiene_expectations.csv` aligned with CMDB owners. (2) Schedule Tenable import hourly. (3) Weekly remediation campaign emails via ServiceNow. (4) Escalate chronic failures to service director. (5) Board deck uses summarized `hygiene_failures`. (6) Sign exports with `evidenceSigning`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Tenable.sc ‚â•6.2 exporting `tenable:sc:assets` through Splunk Add-on ([Splunkbase 4060](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4060)) using modular input interval **900 seconds** (balance API load).
‚Ä¢ Gold lookup `nis2_asset_hygiene_expectations.csv` with mandated controls per `business_service` vetted by CISO office quarterly.
‚Ä¢ Splunk Add-on for Windows ([Splunkbase 742](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)) only if supplementing MFA evidence from Event 4624 claims (optional).
‚Ä¢ Splunk Add-on for Okta ([Splunkbase 6553](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6553)) for SaaS-only environments.
‚Ä¢ License estimate: 2 KB per asset per hour ‚Üí ~1.5 GB/month for 25k assets.

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
‚Ä¢ **Tenable input**: configure credentials under **Apps ‚Üí Tenable Add-on for Splunk ‚Üí Configuration ‚Üí Account** with API keys scoped `access:manage` read-only.
‚Ä¢ Map fields via `props.conf` `EXTRACT-plugins_text = plugins.+(?P<plugins_text>.+)` if JSON nested.
‚Ä¢ Maintain CSV in Git; Splunk Deployment Server pushes new file nightly.
‚Ä¢ Validate `dns_name` resolution matches CMDB `fqdn` using `| join dns_name [search index=cmdb ...]` smoke test.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Use `spl` field; add `| stats sum(hygiene_failures) as total_fails by business_service` for executive chart.
Schedule alert if `total_fails>50`.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
‚Ä¢ Random five assets: open **Tenable.sc ‚Üí Assets ‚Üí Asset Details** and compare plugin text snippet to Splunk `_raw` excerpt.
‚Ä¢ Confirm join cardinality `| stats dc(asset_fqdn)` before/after equals expectation.
‚Ä¢ For Okta augmentation, ensure correlation ID between asset `owner_email` and Okta factor enrollment.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
‚Ä¢ ServiceNow integration: alert action calls `POST /api/now/import/u_hygiene_queue`.
‚Ä¢ Dashboard includes filter tokens for `business_service`.
‚Ä¢ Monthly narrative to risk committee references signed CSV (`evidenceSigning`).

Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshooting
‚Ä¢ **join timeout** ‚Äî precompute summary `summary_hygiene_nightly` via `collect`.
‚Ä¢ **False EDR_ok** ‚Äî vendor renamed product string; update regex table `nis2_edr_markers.csv` external to SPL.
‚Ä¢ **CSV stale** ‚Äî Git webhook failure; monitor `| inputlookup` freshness via `metadata`.
‚Ä¢ **Zero Tenable events** ‚Äî API lockout; tail `ta_tenable.log`.


## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_asset_hygiene_expectations.csv
| join asset_fqdn [
    search index=tenable sourcetype="tenable:sc:assets" earliest=-1d
    | eval mfa_ok=if(match(plugins_text,"(?i)mfa|okta|hello|yubi"),1,0)
    | eval edr_ok=if(match(plugins_text,"(?i)edr|defender|crowdstrike|sentinel"),1,0)
    | stats latest(mfa_ok) as mfa_ok latest(edr_ok) as edr_ok by dns_name
    | rename dns_name as asset_fqdn
]
| eval hygiene_failures=if(mfa_ok==0,1,0) + if(edr_ok==0,1,0)
| where hygiene_failures > 0
| table asset_fqdn, business_service, mfa_ok, edr_ok, hygiene_failures
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest, Vulnerabilities.severity | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Treemap by business_service, bar of failure counts, single-value fleet compliance.
